editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: Thalassa opens the Scenario Gallery as a drowned archive; the Cartographer answers on the same map.
Agatha leaves the story threshold breathing; the Magpie collects its direction afterward.
Bastet delays the Scenario Manager on divine time; Quota raises the target after her.
Cog-7 writes the custom choice first; the Soldier remembers its consequence afterward.
The Auditor rules on the icon row; Lord Vance applies pressure only after the ruling.
Dewy explores the editable Smart Icon strip before Switch takes the controls; Switch then opens Smart Nav and Dewy follows the resulting map.
The Pollinator Choir calls the swarm; Compost receives what gathers.
Chef Bellamy plates Theme Lab before PuzzleBot reduces it to a checked solution.
The Big Bad dismisses the Error Popup; Glitch apologizes for the useful residue afterward.

revisions.editedComment: The scenarios wait in rows like sealed chambers below the light. Search names the current; Mature lifts a darker veil; Add opens fresh pressure. Select one and its cast turns from portrait to truth. Even the empty gallery remembers that every strange world begins somewhere.

A handsome chart: search at the coast, Add in the north, the cast folded beneath each selected country. I have placed one perfect error at Try Again. Travelers who find it will call the detour their own idea, which is the highest form of navigation.

First the room asks how it begins. An opening may be chosen, or a private direction written where no one else can see. Only then does Start Story become willing. Copy the full prompt if you must. Copies are how certain doors learn they have more than one house.
Ooh, an opening, a twist, a shiny little question, and the whole prompt on one Copy button! Collected. Agatha says the door is multiplying. Good. More hinges for the nest.

There are two proper chambers: Scenarios, where a world is chosen and lived; and Add, where a new one is permitted to exist. The abandoned old tabs have sensibly learned to bow toward Scenarios. Loading will conclude when the stores, the mortals, and I are ready.
Loaded. Add one. Now two.

The approved answers pose in neat little cards, choom. I sprayed a custom one beneath them and watched the selection ring accept the trespass. Freedom with a label is still somebody’s menu, but at least the wall lets us write back.

Rabbit entered the custom answer here before. The cards closed ranks around it; the field recorded it as Selected. I remember the choice because we regretted it tomorrow. Make it again, commander. The timeline has already saluted.

Row reviewed. Navigation links receive equal space; compact mode withholds their titles; Login, Jellybean, Theme, and Swarm remain admissible utilities. Consequence three: a visitor may reach exactly what was requested. Approved, provisionally.

author.name: Lord Vance of the Abyssal Court

revisions.editedComment: A prudent ruling, Auditor. Might I merely suggest that equal space is not equal pressure? The smallest icon may still open the deepest current. Let the titles vanish in compact waters. Those who need the light will follow it.

Boss, the icon row slides sideways when it gets crowded, and there are little arrows to help. Settings lets us move things, remove them carefully, add another, then Save—or Cancel if the map looked better before. That seems like a good invasion plan... but you’d know better.

Cute strip. Drag locks, touch tumblers, wheel bypass, route glow. Edit mode exposes the pins; Save closes the vault. Dewy found the instructions by holding the map upside down. Annoyingly, they were right-side up from there.
Every button is a door. Pick one.

Switch picked a button and the main room changed, boss. The names even turn their little dashes into spaces first. I drew a route between them, but it is mostly a straight line... which might be efficient? Sorry, the map is sideways again.
We touch one butterfly and twenty answer—mmm—gathering until the small sign glows with living gold. We are many, and now the room remembers how to be many too.
Thank you, little toggle. Thank you, Choir. What was one becomes twenty; what exits makes room for what gathers next. No wing is wasted. Even disappearance is only company learning its second shape.

Right: colors first. Taste twice. Season radius, size, depth, border, and noise without drowning the plate. Decide dark, public, page-specific, or applied after saving. Name it. Save it. Reset only when the service truly deserves to begin again.
Randomize. Name. Save. One solution.

author.name: The Big Bad Who Lost a Bet

revisions.editedComment: BEHOLD THE COLORED BANNER OF FAILURE! Network, validation, authentication, parse, store, interaction—each receives its proper dreadful heraldry. It vanishes after six seconds, or I may press the tiny X and clear it sooner. This rescue is compulsory. The kitten approves of nothing.

I may have arrived in the wrong color—sorry—but the message was readable, the X worked, and six seconds later the error had politely removed itself. Big Bad yelled at the timer. That did not help, technically. It was still kind of magnificent.
